aids.title: CUHH - Session Tracking Mechanisms
aids.content: **C**ookies (JSESSIONID), **U**RL Rewriting, **H**idden form fields, **H**ttpSession API. CUHH - four ways to track sessions in Java Servlets.

aids.title: Session SARI Methods
aids.content: **S**etAttribute, **g**etAttribute, **R**emoveAttribute, **I**nvalidate. SARI - the essential HttpSession methods for managing session data.

aids.title: getSession() vs getSession(false)
aids.content: getSession() = Always returns a session (creates if needed). getSession(false) = Returns null if no session exists. Think: 'false means do NOT force creation.'

aids.title: Session Timeout Units
aids.content: In web.xml: minutes. In code (setMaxInactiveInterval): seconds. Remember: 'XML is Macro (minutes), Code is micro (seconds).'
aids.forRemembering: Session timeout units in web.xml vs code

aids.title: URL Rewriting Analogy
aids.content: URL Rewriting is like wearing a name badge at a conference. If you refuse cookies (name stickers), the server puts your ID in the URL (stamped on your badge) so it can recognize you.
aids.forRemembering: Why URL rewriting is used as a cookie fallback
